name: Apple Turnovers
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. In a bowl, mix flour and salt. Add shortening and cut in until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Mix in cheese and about 3 tablespoons of water (adjust as needed), then combine with fork until dough forms a ball.
3. Divide dough into three equal parts. Roll each part on a lightly floured surface into a 14 x 7-inch rectangle. Cut each into two 7-inch squares.
4. In a bowl, mix brown sugar and cinnamon. Fill the centers of apples in each square with the mixture, then top each apple with butter. Sprinkle remaining brown sugar mixture over pastry.
5. Place an apple in each square and fold corners to center, pinching dough at the top of the apple to seal.
6. Bake for 30-35 minutes. Top with whipped cream.
